fixtures.description: An indefinitely extensible linear reasoning history with finite dependency cones and monotone prefix identity.
An effectively indexed branching deliberation whose selected run exposes every finite prefix and preserves rejected alternatives as history.
An unbounded history containing finitely supported revisions and rule changes with explicit before-and-after dependency records.
A fixed finite horizon is asserted to represent the entire open-ended history and loses a later revision.
The constructor reads future events to encode the current prefix or decide present admissibility.
A step requires an unrecoverable property of the entire infinite past and has no finite dependency certificate.
One revision rewrites an unbounded past without a finite effective change certificate.
